budReference.title: BUD Quick Reference — USP 797 (2023 Revised)
budReference.note: BUD is assigned at time of compounding. Storage condition changes after compounding do NOT extend BUD.

categories.label: Immediate-Use CSP
requirements: ≤3 sterile starting components AND ≤2 entries per container
Administration MUST begin within 4 hours of preparation start (USP 797 2023 — extended from 1 h legacy)

bud.roomTemp: Admin start ≤ 4 hours from prep start

categories.label: Category 1 CSP
categories.environmentRequired: Prepared in ISO 5 PEC located in a Segregated Compounding Area (SCA) — NOT within a full cleanroom suite

categories.label: Category 2 CSP (no sterility testing)
categories.environmentRequired: ISO 5 PEC within ISO 7 buffer room + ISO 8 anteroom cleanroom suite

categories.label: Category 2 CSP (passing sterility test)
categories.environmentRequired: ISO 5 PEC within ISO 7 buffer room + ISO 8 anteroom cleanroom suite

categories.label: Category 3 CSP (extended-stability)
categories.environmentRequired: ISO 5 PEC within ISO 7 buffer + ISO 8 anteroom cleanroom suite WITH enhanced controls — additional monitoring frequency, more stringent personnel qualification, and mandatory release testing
requirements: Sterility testing per USP <71> (passing) — required, not optional
Bacterial endotoxin testing per USP <85> (passing) — required
Container–closure integrity testing for the formulation
Stability data supporting the assigned BUD
Personnel re-qualification every 3 months (vs 6 for Cat 2)
More frequent environmental monitoring than Category 2
categories.note: Category 3 BUDs are not defaults — the assigned BUD must be supported by documented testing AND stability evidence for that exact formulation. Introduced in USP 797 (2023 revised).

criticalDistinctions: Sterility testing extends BUD for Category 2 and 3 ONLY — not Category 1
Category 3 (NEW in 2023) requires sterility, endotoxin, container-closure integrity, AND stability data — testing alone is insufficient without supporting stability evidence
Nonaqueous = oils, anhydrous vehicles (some ophthalmics, certain injections in oil)
Category 2 or 3 BUDs cannot be applied to CSPs prepared in an SCA — this is the most common real-world compliance error
Immediate-use BUD is ≤4 hours from start of preparation (changed in 2023 revision; legacy was 1 hour)
BUD ≠ expiration date — BUD is assigned at compounding; it cannot be extended by changing storage conditions after the fact
Multi-dose vials (commercially manufactured) are NOT subject to USP 797 BUD rules — defer to manufacturer labeling

classes.particleLimit: ≤3,520 particles/m³ at ≥0.5 µm; ≤29 particles/m³ at ≥5 µm
classes.role: Direct compounding surface — all critical sites must remain in this airstream

examples: Sterile compounding room housing the PEC
classes.hvac: ≥30 ACPH total air changes; ≥15 ACPH from HEPA-filtered supply
classes.role: Houses the PEC. Required to achieve Category 2 BUDs. Must be positive pressure relative to anteroom
classes.certificationFrequency: Every 6 months by qualified Certifying Organization

examples: Material staging area adjacent to buffer room
classes.hvac: Positive pressure relative to general pharmacy; negative pressure relative to buffer room is acceptable for hazardous drug suites
classes.role: Garbing and material staging. SCA uses a non-classified space (no ISO rating) — limits compounding to Category 1 BUDs only

sca.label: Segregated Compounding Area (SCA)
sca.description: ISO 5 PEC placed in a non-classified (no ISO rating) room or designated area
sca.certificationRequired: PEC still must be certified every 6 months
sca.commonMistake: Assigning Category 2 BUDs (45 days, etc.) to CSPs prepared in an SCA — this is a critical compliance failure regardless of hood quality

cleanroom.label: Full Cleanroom Suite
cleanroom.description: ISO 5 PEC inside an ISO 7 buffer room connected to an ISO 8 anteroom with required pressure differentials and ACPH
cleanroom.maxBUD: Category 2 eligible — up to 90 days room temp (with sterility testing) or 180 days (nonaqueous with sterility testing)
cleanroom.certificationRequired: Every 6 months — particle counts, airflow velocity, HEPA filter integrity, pressure differentials
